facts.statement: Session initialization — no issue domain identified yet
No party positions or interests articulated
There is an active dispute about school-night overnights.
The child has school-related routine needs that both parents recognize as important.
STRUCTURAL: Party B has not spoken — this is not an information gap about logistics, it is the absence of a required stakeholder voice
PROCEDURAL: Standard mediation practice requires both parties' opening statements before problem-solving
Parent B links school-week overnights to meaningful parenting-role concerns.
facts.note: Accepted as a stated process-relevant concern, not as an adjudicated conclusion about intent.

facts.statement: Cannot assess feasibility of any structural option (gradual expansion, midweek visits, transition protocols) without knowing current baseline schedule
Child's age/stage determines what overnight frequency and transition patterns are developmentally appropriate
Transport, exchange timing, and homework-routine reliability remain unresolved for school-week overnights.
facts.note: The session surfaced these items as unresolved rather than settled.

facts.statement: Commute timing feasibility (moderate gap — no specifics on distance or current logistics)
Exchange punctuality as trust/reliability proxy (both parties named this)
Both parents accept clarification of logistics as the bounded next step.

facts_notes: Uncertain feasibility items remain explicit rather than being promoted to settled conclusions.
